title: Low profile head-located neurostimulator and method of fabrication
abstract: A method for subcutaneously treating pain in a patient includes first providing a neurostimulator with an IPG body and at least a primary, a secondary, and a tertiary integral lead with electrodes disposed thereon. A primary incision is opened to expose the subcutaneous region below the dermis in a selected portion of the body. A pocket is then opened for the IPG through the primary incision and the integral leads are inserted through the primary incision and routed subcutaneously to desired nerve regions along desired paths. The IPG is disposed in the pocket through the primary incision. The primary incision is then closed and the IPG and the electrodes activated to provide localized stimulation to the desired nerve regions and at least three of the nerves associated therewith to achieve a desired pain reduction response from the patient.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A low profile implantable pulse generator comprising: a battery; a printed circuit board; a generally rectangular metal can enclosing the printed circuit board and the battery, the can including a top piece having a top side with a recessed portion having an interior side wall and an open side at a first end of the can; a plurality of cut-outs formed in the recessed portion, a coil positioned adjacent the first end of the metal can, the coil operable to charge the battery; and a plurality of leads extending from the first end of the can proximate to and across the coil, each of the leads including a plurality of wires, the wires extending though the open side of the recessed portion below the plane defined by top side of the can, a plurality of the wires transmitting low frequency pulses from the printed circuit board to a selected frontal, parietal, or occipital nerve region. 2. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 1 wherein the coil has a substantially linear first side proximate the first end of the can and a substantially semicircular second side extending away from the first end of the can. 3. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 2 wherein the substantially linear first side of the coil extends substantially parallel to the edge of the first side of the can. 4. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 2 wherein the leads extend from the first end of the can proximate to and across the substantially linear first side of the coil and proximate to and across the semicircular second side of the coil. 5. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 2 further comprising a feedthrough positioned over each of the cutouts, the feedthrough comprising a substantially flat metal plate portion with a plurality of ceramic rings extending through the flat metal plate portion, each of the ceramic rings having a conductive pin extending through the ring, the pins extending substantially perpendicular to the flat metal plate portion and wherein the 

 wires are connected to the pins to extend substantially perpendicular to the pins below the plane defined by top side of the can. 6. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 2 wherein the pulse generator is encapsulated in silicone. 7. A low profile implantable pulse generator comprising: a battery; a printed circuit board; a generally rectangular metal can enclosing the printed circuit board and the battery, the can including a top piece having a top side defining a plane with a recessed portion at a first end of the can, the recessed portion having an interior side wall and an open side at the first end of the can and a substantially flat bottom piece welded to the top piece; a plurality of substantially oval cut-outs formed in the recessed portion, a plurality of feedthroughs in the cutouts; a coil positioned adjacent the first end of the metal can, the coil operable to receive an RF transmission and charge the battery, the coil having a substantially linear first side proximate the first end of the can and a substantially semicircular second side extending away from the first end of the can; and a plurality of leads extending from the first end of the can proximate to and across the coil, each of the leads including a plurality of wires, the wires extending though the open side of the recessed portion below the plane defined by top side of the can, a plurality of the wires transmitting low frequency pulses from the printed circuit board to a selected supraorbital, infraorbital, auricular-temporal or occipital nerve region. 8. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 7 wherein a plurality of the wires couple the coil to the battery. 9. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 8 further comprising a feedthrough positioned over each of the cutouts, the feedthrough comprising a substantially flat metal plate portion with a plurality of ceramic rings extending through the flat metal plate portion, each of the ceramic rings having a conductive pin extending through the ring, the 

 pins extending substantially perpendicular to the flat metal plate portion and wherein the wires are connected to the pins to extend substantially perpendicular to the pins below the plane defined by top side of the can. 10. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 9 wherein a first one of the feedthroughs connects the coil to the printed circuit board and second ones of the feedthroughs connect the wires in the leads to the printed circuit board. 11. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 9 wherein the first feedthrough and the second feedthroughs are positioned at the first end of the can with the first feedthrough proximate one of the second feedthroughs. 12. The low profile low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 9 wherein the substantially linear first side of the coil extends substantially parallel to the edge of the first side of the can. 13. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 9 wherein the pulse generator is encapsulated in silicone with the silicone forming a center portion encapsulating the can and angled lobes at each end of the can wherein the coil is encapsulated in one of the lobes at the first end of the center section. 14. The low profile implantable pulse generator of Claim 11 wherein the lobes are angled downward from about 10 to about 20 degrees from the center section. 15. A low profile implantable pulse generator comprising: a battery; a printed circuit board; a generally rectangular metal can enclosing the printed circuit board and the battery, the can including a top piece having a top side defining a plane with a recessed portion at a first end of the can, the recessed portion having an interior side wall and an open side at the first end of the can and a substantially flat bottom piece welded to the top piece; a plurality of substantially oval cut-outs formed in the recessed portion, a plurality of feedthroughs in the cutouts; 

 a coil positioned adjacent the first end of the metal can, the coil operable to receive an RF transmission and charge the battery, the coil having a substantially linear first side proximate the first end of the can and a substantially semicircular second side extending away from the first end of the can; and a plurality of leads extending from the first end of the can proximate to and across the coil, each of the leads including a plurality of wires, the wires extending though the open side of the recessed portion below the plane defined by top side of the can, a plurality of the wires transmitting low frequency pulses from the printed circuit board to a selected supraorbital, infraorbital, auricular-temporal or occipital nerve region. 16. The low profile implantable pulse generator of claim 15 wherein the pulse generator is encapsulated in silicone with the silicone forming a center portion encapsulating the can and angled lobes at each end of the can wherein the coil is encapsulated in one of the lobes at the first end of the center section with at least a portion of one of the leads encapsulated in the silicone and extending proximate to and across the coil. 17. The low profile implantable pulse generator of claim 16 wherein the lobes are angled downward from about 10 to about 20 degrees from the center section.
